value.claim: A deep residual learning framework is proposed that reformulates layers to learn residual functions with reference to layer inputs, easing the training of substantially deeper networks.
Residual networks are easier to optimize than plain networks, as evidenced by lower training errors in deep configurations where plain networks exhibit degradation.
An ensemble of 152-layer residual nets achieves 3.57% top-5 error on the ImageNet test set, winning 1st place on the ILSVRC 2015 classification task.
Deep residual nets achieve a 28% relative improvement on the COCO object detection dataset solely due to their extremely deep representations.

The residual learning principle is generic and applicable beyond image classification, as demonstrated by winning 1st places on ImageNet detection, localization, COCO detection, and COCO segmentation.
